name: Context Assembly
description: How AI decisions are guided by assembling many small text snippets into context

content.title: Context Assembly: The Key to AI Intelligence
content.content:
# Context Assembly: The Key to AI Intelligence

The secret to effective AI is not model training - it's **context assembly**. AI makes intelligent decisions when it has the right information in its working memory at the right time.

## The Core Insight

AI decisions are guided by assembling **many small text snippets** into context. Each decision point pulls in a different contextual piece - instructions, rules, examples, definitions.

## Example: Processing an Incoming Email

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Email Arrives] --> B{What type of email?}
    B --> |Context: Email Classification Guide| C[Claims Report]
    
    C --> D{Which claim?}
    D --> |Context: Claim Reference Extraction Rules| E[CLM-12345]
    
    E --> F{What line of business?}
    F --> |Context: LoB Detection Keywords| G[Casualty]
    
    G --> H{How to process?}
    H --> |Context: Casualty Claims Handling Manual| I[Process Steps]
    
    I --> J{Needs escalation?}
    J --> |Context: Escalation Rules for Casualty| K[Decision]
```

## Many Small Texts

At each decision point, the AI pulls in a specific contextual text:

| Decision | Context Text Pulled In |
|----------|------------------------|
| "Is this a claims report?" | Email type classification guide |
| "Which claim does this reference?" | Claim reference extraction patterns |
| "What line of business?" | LoB keywords and definitions |
| "How should casualty claims be handled?" | Casualty claims processing manual |
| "Does this need escalation?" | Escalation thresholds and rules |

Each text is small - perhaps 100-500 words. But together, they form a comprehensive instruction set.

## The Context Window

Modern AI models have a "context window" - a working memory where all relevant information must fit:

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "Context Window (Working Memory)"
        A[Email Content]
        B[Classification Guide]
        C[Claim History]
        D[LoB Definitions]
        E[Processing Rules]
        F[Escalation Thresholds]
    end
    
    G[AI Model] --> |reads all| A
    G --> B
    G --> C
    G --> D
    G --> E
    G --> F
    G --> H[Intelligent Decision]
```

## Why This Approach Works

**Not training, but context:**
- AI models don't need to be trained on your specific business
- They need the right context at decision time
- Small, focused texts are easier to maintain than training data
- Changes take effect immediately - no retraining needed

**Addressability enables assembly:**
- Each context snippet is addressable in the data mesh
- AI can request exactly the context it needs
- Relevant snippets are assembled dynamically
- Context stays current as source texts are updated

## Building Your Context Library

Effective AI requires building a library of contextual texts:

1. **Classification guides** - How to identify types
2. **Extraction rules** - What to look for and where
3. **Business definitions** - What terms mean in your domain
4. **Process instructions** - How to handle each scenario
5. **Escalation rules** - When to involve humans

This is the work that makes AI effective - not model tuning, but knowledge capture.
